dialogues: Two different ways to prove the same thing. Highlighting: you read the same array two ways. Rotation: you turn the array and rows become columns. Either way, same dots, same product.
This doesn't just work for these arrays. It works for every array. Any array you can build. The two expressions will always give the same product. Always.
Right. Same dots. Whether you read them by rows or columns, whether you turn the array, the dots don't change. That's why the product can't change.
